core_mechanism: statistical error reduction of a large measurement catalog by cross-calibrating raw observations against an external reference standard and coadding repeated overlapping measurements, so that random (rms) error falls and the detection threshold is pushed below the single-measurement noise floor
brief_reason: This is a data-release descriptor rather than a paper built on one governing equation, so the most specific defensible machinery is its calibration/aggregation pipeline: recalibrating astrometry against a reference catalog (UCAC-2) to cut rms errors and coadding repeat photometry to reach ~2 magnitudes fainter.
